neoterra.gui.button.climateSeedOffset.to: A seed offset used to randomise climate distribution
neoterra.gui.button.continentShape.toolt: Controls how continent shapes are calculated. You may also need to adjust the transition points to ensure beaches etc still form.
Use custom biome features in place of vanilla ones (such as trees)
neoterra.gui.button.erosionDecorator.too: Replace surface materials where erosion has occurred
neoterra.gui.button.fancyMountains.toolt: Carries out extra processing on mountains to make them look even nicer. Can be disabled to improve performance slightly.
neoterra.gui.button.largeOreVeins.toolti: Set whether large ore veins spawn
neoterra.gui.button.legacyMountainScalin: Uses legacy mountain scaling for compatibility with older presets
neoterra.gui.button.naturalSnowDecorator: Removes snow from the terrain where it shouldn't naturally settle
neoterra.gui.button.oreCompatibleStoneOn: Only use stone types that ores can generate in
neoterra.gui.button.renderMode.tooltip: Selects how the preview map is colored
neoterra.gui.button.riverSeedOffset.tool: A seed offset used to randomise river distribution
neoterra.gui.button.salt.tooltip: A random seed value for the structure.
neoterra.gui.button.smoothLayerDecorator: Modifies layer block levels (ie snow) to fit the terrain
neoterra.gui.button.spawnType.tooltip: Set whether spawn should be close to x=0,z=0 or the centre of the nearest continent
neoterra.gui.button.stoneErosion.tooltip: Changes most exposed rock surfaces to plain stone
neoterra.gui.button.strataDecorator.tool: Generates strata (rock layers) instead of just stone
neoterra.gui.button.terrainSeedOffset.to: A seed offset used to randomise terrain distribution
neoterra.gui.button.vanillaLavaSprings.t: Allow vanilla springs (lava source blocks) to generate
neoterra.gui.button.vanillaSprings.toolt: Allow vanilla springs (water source blocks) to generate
neoterra.gui.caveSettings.title: Cave Settings (Experimental)
neoterra.gui.climateSettings.title: Climate Settings
neoterra.gui.filterSettings.title: Filter Settings
neoterra.gui.miscellaneousSettings.title: Miscellaneous Settings
neoterra.gui.preset.beautiful.name: Beautiful (Legacy)
neoterra.gui.preset.default.name: Default
neoterra.gui.preset.hugeBiomes.name: Huge Biomes (Legacy)
neoterra.gui.preset.legacyDefault.name: Default (Legacy)
neoterra.gui.preset.lite.name: Lite (Legacy)
neoterra.gui.preset.vanillaish.name: Vanilla-ish (Legacy)
neoterra.gui.riverSettings.title: River Settings
neoterra.gui.selectPreset.title: Presets & Defaults
neoterra.gui.slider.beach.tooltip: Controls how much of the coastal terrain is assigned to beach biomes.
neoterra.gui.slider.biomeEdgeGain.toolti: Controls the gain subsequent noise octaves
Controls the lacunarity of subsequent noise octaves
neoterra.gui.slider.biomeEdgeOctaves.too: Controls the number of noise octaves
neoterra.gui.slider.biomeEdgeScale.toolt: Controls the scale of the noise
neoterra.gui.slider.biomeEdgeStrength.to: Controls the strength of the noise
neoterra.gui.slider.biomeSize.tooltip: Controls the size of individual biomes
neoterra.gui.slider.biomeWarpScale.toolt: Controls the scale of shape distortion for biomes
neoterra.gui.slider.biomeWarpStrength.to: Controls the strength of shape distortion for biomes
neoterra.gui.slider.carverCaveProbabilit: Controls the probability that a cave carver will generate
neoterra.gui.slider.cheeseCaveDepthOffse: Controls the depth at which cheese caves start to generate
neoterra.gui.slider.cheeseCaveProbabilit: Controls probability that a cheese cave will generate
neoterra.gui.slider.coast.tooltip: Controls the size of coastal regions and is also the point below which inland terrain transitions into oceans.
neoterra.gui.slider.continentJitter.tool: Controls how much continent centers are offset from the underlying noise grid.
neoterra.gui.slider.continentNoiseGain.t: The contribution strength of each noise octave.
neoterra.gui.slider.continentNoiseLacuna: The frequency multiplier for each noise octave.
neoterra.gui.slider.continentNoiseOctave: The number of octaves of noise used to distort the continent.
neoterra.gui.slider.continentScale.toolt: Controls the size of continents. You may also need to adjust the transition points to ensure beaches etc still form.
neoterra.gui.slider.continentSizeVarianc: Increases the variance of continent sizes.
neoterra.gui.slider.continentSkipping.to: Reduces the number of continents to create more vast oceans.
neoterra.gui.slider.deepCarverCaveProbab: Controls the probability that a deep cave carver will generate
neoterra.gui.slider.deepOcean.tooltip: Controls the point above which deep oceans transition into shallow oceans. The greater the gap to the shallow ocean slider, the more gradual the transition.
neoterra.gui.slider.dirtMin.tooltip: Controls the y level at which the surface can transition into dirt
neoterra.gui.slider.dirtSteepness.toolti: The steepness at which the surface can transition into dirt
neoterra.gui.slider.dirtVariance.tooltip: Controls the density of the dirt gradient
neoterra.gui.slider.entranceCaveProbabil: Controls the probability that an entrance cave will generate
neoterra.gui.slider.erosion.depositeRate: Controls how quickly material is deposited (during erosion)
neoterra.gui.slider.erosion.dropletLifet: Controls the number of iterations that a single water droplet is simulated for
neoterra.gui.slider.erosion.dropletVeloc: Controls the starting velocity of the simulated water droplet
neoterra.gui.slider.erosion.dropletVolum: Controls the starting volume of water that a simulated water droplet carries
neoterra.gui.slider.erosion.dropletsPerC: The average number of water droplets to simulate per chunk
neoterra.gui.slider.erosion.rate.tooltip: Controls how quickly material dissolves (during erosion)
neoterra.gui.slider.globalHorizontalScal: Globally controls the horizontal scaling of terrain
Globally controls the vertical scaling of terrain
neoterra.gui.slider.inland.tooltip: Controls the overall transition from ocean to inland terrain.
neoterra.gui.slider.islandCoast.tooltip: Controls the point below which deep oceans transition into island coasts. The greater the gap to the deep ocean slider, the more gradual the transition.
neoterra.gui.slider.islandInland.tooltip: Controls the point below which island coasts transition into island. The greater the gap to the island coasts slider, the more gradual the transition.
neoterra.gui.slider.lake.chance.tooltip: Controls the chance of a lake spawning
neoterra.gui.slider.lake.depth.tooltip: The max depth of the lake
neoterra.gui.slider.lake.maxStartDistanc: The maximum distance along a river that a lake will spawn
neoterra.gui.slider.lake.minStartDistanc: The minimum distance along a river that a lake will spawn
neoterra.gui.slider.lake.sizeMax.tooltip: The maximum size of the lake
neoterra.gui.slider.lake.sizeMin.tooltip: The minimum size of the lake
neoterra.gui.slider.macroNoiseSize.toolt: Macro noise is used to group large areas of biomes into a single type (such as deserts)
neoterra.gui.slider.moistureBias.tooltip: The bias towards either end of the range
neoterra.gui.slider.moistureFalloff.tool: How quickly values transition from an extremity
neoterra.gui.slider.moistureMax.tooltip: The upper limit of the range
neoterra.gui.slider.moistureMin.tooltip: The lower limit of the range
neoterra.gui.slider.mountainBiomeUsage.t: The probability that mountainous terrain will be set to a mountain biome type.\nThis may help improve compatibility with mods that rely exclusively on mountain biomes.
neoterra.gui.slider.noodleCaveProbabilit: Controls probability that a noodle cave will generate
neoterra.gui.slider.ravineProbability.to: Controls the probability that a ravine carver will generate
neoterra.gui.slider.river.bedDepth.toolt: Controls the depth of the river
Controls the height of river banks
Controls the height of river banks
neoterra.gui.slider.riverCount.tooltip: Controls the number of main rivers per continent.
neoterra.gui.slider.rockMin.tooltip: Controls the y level at which the surface can transition into rock
neoterra.gui.slider.rockSteepness.toolti: The steepness at which the surface can transition into rock
neoterra.gui.slider.rockVariance.tooltip: Controls the density of the rock gradient
neoterra.gui.slider.screeSteepness.toolt: The steepness at which the surface can transition into gravel
neoterra.gui.slider.separation.tooltip: Controls the minimum distance between instances of the structure. Larger values guarantee larger distances between structures of the same type but cause them to generate more 'grid-aligned'. Lower values will produce a more random distribution but may allow instances to generate closer together.
neoterra.gui.slider.shallowOcean.tooltip: Controls the point above which shallow oceans transition into coastal terrain. The greater the gap to the coast slider, the more gradual the transition.
neoterra.gui.slider.smoothing.iterations: Controls the number of smoothing iterations
neoterra.gui.slider.smoothing.rate.toolt: Controls how strongly smoothing is applied
neoterra.gui.slider.spacing.tooltip: Controls the size of the grid used to generate the structure. Structures will attempt to generate once per grid cell. Larger spacing values will make the structure appear less frequently.
neoterra.gui.slider.spaghettiCaveProbabi: Controls the probability that a spaghetti cave will generate
neoterra.gui.slider.strataRegionSize.too: Controls the size of strata regions
neoterra.gui.slider.temperatureBias.tool: The bias towards either end of the range
neoterra.gui.slider.temperatureFalloff.t: How quickly values transition from an extremity
neoterra.gui.slider.temperatureMax.toolt: The upper limit of the range
neoterra.gui.slider.temperatureMin.toolt: The lower limit of the range
neoterra.gui.slider.terrain.baseScale.to: Controls the base height of this terrain
neoterra.gui.slider.terrain.horizontalSc: Stretches or compresses the terrain horizontally
neoterra.gui.slider.terrain.verticalScal: Stretches or compresses the terrain vertically
neoterra.gui.slider.terrain.weight.toolt: Controls how common this terrain type is
neoterra.gui.slider.terrainRegionSize.to: Controls the size of terrain regions
neoterra.gui.slider.volcanoBiomeUsage.to: The probability that volcano terrain will be set to a volcano biome type.\nThis may help improve compatibility with mods that rely exclusively on volcano biomes.
neoterra.gui.slider.wetland.sizeMax.tool: The maximum size of the wetlands
neoterra.gui.slider.wetland.sizeMin.tool: The minimum size of the wetlands
neoterra.gui.slider.zoom.tooltip: Controls the zoom level of the preview map
neoterra.gui.structureSettings.title: Structure Settings
neoterra.gui.surfaceSettings.title: Surface Settings (Experimental)
neoterra.gui.terrainSettings.title: Terrain Settings
neoterra.gui.worldSettings.title: World Settings
neoterra.mudSwamps.metadata.description: Changes the swamp material to mud
